blocks.body: Present Simple: Negatives — do not, does not, don't, doesn't i podstawowa forma czasownika.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"W przeczeniach Present Simple stawiamy do not / does not przed czasownikiem głównym.","rules":[{"label":"I / You / We / They don't","color":"emerald","text":"Z tymi podmiotami używaj do not albo don't.","example":"I don't work on Sundays."},{"label":"He / She / It doesn't","color":"blue","text":"Z he / she / it używaj does not albo doesn't.","example":"She doesn't like coffee."},{"label":"Base verb after do / does","color":"amber","text":"Po do / does czasownik wraca do formy podstawowej.","example":"He doesn't go, not he doesn't goes."}]}

{"title":"1. Wzór przeczenia","intro":"Zapamiętaj dwa główne schematy.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"do not + V1","subtitle":"I do not work here. / We don't live near the station."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"does not + V1","subtitle":"He does not play tennis. / She doesn't drive."},{"label":"Full forms","title":"do not / does not","subtitle":"Pełne formy brzmią trochę bardziej formalnie albo wyraźniej."},{"label":"Short forms","title":"don't / doesn't","subtitle":"W codziennym języku skróty są bardzo częste."}]}

{"title":"2. Jak to działa w zdaniu","sections":[{"label":"Nawyki","color":"emerald","description":"Powiedz, czego zwykle nie robisz.","examples":[{"en":"I don't drink coffee at night.","ua":"Nie piję kawy w nocy."},{"en":"They don't walk to school.","ua":"Nie chodzą do szkoły pieszo."}]},{"label":"He / She / It","color":"blue","description":"W trzeciej osobie liczby pojedynczej używaj doesn't.","examples":[{"en":"He doesn't watch TV in the morning.","ua":"On nie ogląda telewizji rano."},{"en":"My sister doesn't eat meat.","ua":"Moja siostra nie je mięsa."}]},{"label":"Twierdzenie vs przeczenie","color":"amber","description":"Porównaj zdanie twierdzące i przeczące: w przeczeniu czasownik stoi w V1.","examples":[{"en":"She works here. → She doesn't work here.","ua":"Po doesn't, works zmienia się na work."},{"en":"We play on Fridays. → We don't play on Fridays.","ua":"Po don't czasownik zostaje w formie podstawowej."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Twierdzenie i przeczenie","intro":"W przeczeniu dodajemy do / does + not.","rows":[{"en":"I work on Sundays.","ua":"I don't work on Sundays.","note":"do + not + forma podstawowa"},{"en":"They like spicy food.","ua":"They don't like spicy food.","note":"don't + like"},{"en":"He plays tennis.","ua":"He doesn't play tennis.","note":"doesn't + play"},{"en":"She goes by bus.","ua":"She doesn't go by bus.","note":"doesn't + go"}],"warning":"Nie mów He doesn't goes."}

{"title":"4. Typowe błędy","items":[{"label":"Błąd 1","color":"rose","title":"Używanie don't z he / she / it.","wrong":"❌ He don't like tea.","right":"✅ He doesn't like tea."},{"label":"Błąd 2","color":"amber","title":"Zostawianie -s po doesn't.","wrong":"❌ She doesn't goes to school by bus.","right":"✅ She doesn't go to school by bus."},{"label":"Błąd 3","color":"sky","title":"Używanie not bez do.","wrong":"❌ I not work on Sundays.","right":"✅ I don't work on Sundays."}]}

{"title":"5. Krótkie podsumowanie","items":["Przeczenie w Present Simple = do / does + not + V1.","Z I / you / we / they używaj don't albo do not.","Z he / she / it używaj doesn't albo does not.","Po do / does zawsze używaj formy podstawowej czasownika.","Typowy błąd to doesn't goes zamiast doesn't go."]}

{"title":"Lekcje Present Simple","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/pl/theory/present-simple/present-simple-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/pl/theory/present-simple/present-simple-negatives","current":true},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/pl/theory/present-simple/present-simple-questions","current":false},{"label":"Adverbs of Frequency","url":"/pl/theory/present-simple/present-simple-adverbs-of-frequency","current":false}]}
